summary: A Go-based test runner and MCP integration framework heavily augmented by AI agent-generated test suites.
concerns: high volume of auto-generated extra tests from glm-agents may bloat the codebase
staged changes left uncommitted indicating potential workflow interruption
deep nesting of agent-generated directories (GOrchestra/glm-agents/...) adds repository noise
potential redundancy between baseline tests and agent-generated extra tests
suggestions: evaluate consolidating or archiving agent-generated test directories to reduce clutter
commit or stash the staged changes to maintain a clean working tree
ensure agent-generated tests are gated by CI to prevent flaky tests from merging
document the glm-agents workflow and its output expectations for future contributors
